name: grief-support
description: Process grief and rebuild at your own pace. Face what you're feeling, build a sustainable daily structure, develop coping habits, write to your future self, and establish a weekly check-in rhythm. Not therapy — structure for when everything feels structureless.

config.instruction: Not fears this time — feelings. Inventory what you're experiencing: grief, anger, guilt, relief, numbness, confusion. For each one, acknowledge it without judgment. Identify which ones are loudest right now and which are hiding underneath.

Build a gentle daily routine. Not productivity — survival. Include: one meal you actually eat, movement (even a walk), one human connection, rest without guilt, and one tiny task that makes tomorrow slightly easier. Low bar. Achievable.

Build 3-5 small coping habits: journaling (even one sentence), a physical anchor (walk, stretch, breathe), a social minimum (one text, one call), a creative outlet, and a gratitude practice that doesn't feel forced. Tiny, sustainable, no pressure.
Write a letter to yourself 6 months from now. What do you want future-you to know about what you're going through? What would you be proud of surviving? What would 'okay' look like?

Set up a weekly check-in template. Not a productivity review — a wellness check. Questions: How am I actually doing (1-10)? Did I eat and sleep? Did I connect with someone? What was hard this week? What was one small good thing? Do I need help?
